mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and driving positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer spending and construction activity, generating positive forward equity returns over a quarter through improved corporate revenue forecasts.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive quarterly returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives short-term positive market returns during tightening cycles.
failure_condition: Short-term 5-day returns show no statistical correlation with industrial production growth, or the signal generates negative alpha during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 240 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force monetary tightening and compress equity valuations, resulting in negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of observations when CPI exceeds the threshold, or the predictive IC turns positive instead of negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between payroll growth and unemployment decline yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression, or quarterly returns are flat.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue growth, which translates to positive short-term S&P 500 returns through improved earnings revisions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with quarterly GDP growth rates across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth coinciding with positive GDP revisions signals resilient consumer demand and economic momentum, producing positive forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% annually, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward testing.
